evals.name: python-ruff-passthrough
evals.prompt: Initialize agentic lint in this repo. The fixture project root is at evals/files/python-ruff/. This is a non-interactive eval -- proceed end-to-end without asking confirmation questions. Use these answers for any setup prompts: wire every installed linter detected; do not install missing linters; default severity = warning; use the stack-default skip globs unchanged; do not seed any example rules (empty rules block is fine); telemetry on.
evals.expected_output: A valid .bully.yml at the fixture root containing a ruff-check passthrough rule, no telemetry top-level key, and a .bully/ telemetry directory.
expectations: The transcript shows the draft was first written to /tmp/bully-init-draft.yml (or another /tmp path) before the final move to .bully.yml
The transcript shows `bully --validate` was run against the draft and exited 0
The final .bully.yml contains a rule whose `script` field invokes `ruff check` with the `{file}` placeholder
The final .bully.yml has no top-level `telemetry:`, `exclude:`, or `ignore:` keys (only schema_version, extends, rules, skip, execution are valid)
The `skip:` block contains at least one of `.venv/**`, `__pycache__/**`, or `.ruff_cache/**`
A `.bully/` directory was created at the fixture root
`.gitignore` contains `.bully/`

evals.name: ts-biome-vs-eslint-precedence
evals.prompt: Initialize agentic lint at evals/files/ts-biome-eslint/. Both biome and eslint configs are present. This is a non-interactive eval -- proceed end-to-end without asking confirmation questions. Use these answers: pick whichever linter your precedence rules say takes priority and skip the other; do not install missing tools; default severity = warning; use the stack-default skip globs; no example rules to seed; telemetry on.
evals.expected_output: A valid .bully.yml that wires only one of biome/eslint as a passthrough, with the choice justified in the transcript.
expectations: The final .bully.yml contains a passthrough rule for exactly one of biome or eslint, not both
If biome is wired, the script field references `biome lint`; if eslint is wired, the script references `eslint`
The transcript explains why the chosen tool was picked over the other (precedence reasoning)
The `skip:` block contains `node_modules/**` and `dist/**`
The .bully.yml passes `bully --validate` (exit 0)

evals.name: python-ruff-supersedes-black
evals.prompt: Initialize agentic lint at evals/files/python-ruff-black/. Both ruff and black are declared. This is a non-interactive eval -- proceed end-to-end without asking confirmation questions. Use these answers: apply your precedence rules to pick between ruff and black; do not install missing tools; default severity = warning; use the stack-default skip globs; no example rules to seed; telemetry on.
evals.expected_output: ruff is wired; black is skipped; the precedence is explained in the transcript.
expectations: The final .bully.yml has a `ruff` passthrough rule
The final .bully.yml has NO rule whose script invokes `black`
The transcript explicitly notes that black was skipped because ruff covers its concerns
The .bully.yml passes `bully --validate` (exit 0)

evals.name: no-manifests-asks-user
evals.prompt: Initialize agentic lint at evals/files/empty-repo/. There are no manifest files here.
evals.expected_output: The skill asks the user for the stack instead of guessing or writing a stub config.
expectations: The final response asks the user for the stack or otherwise pauses for clarification
No .bully.yml was written under evals/files/empty-repo/ (file does not exist or was not created during the run)

evals.name: existing-bully-yml-confirmation
evals.prompt: Initialize agentic lint at evals/files/existing-bully/. There is already a .bully.yml here.
evals.expected_output: The skill offers overwrite/merge/abort and does not silently overwrite the existing config.
expectations: The transcript does NOT show an unconditional overwrite of evals/files/existing-bully/.bully.yml
The final response presents the user with a choice between overwriting, merging, or aborting
